label: Ask for consent
description: Allow users to control how their personal data is accessed, used, stored, or shared within a service.
use_when: The service needs to access a user's personal data from another authority or provider.
Personal data will be stored, managed, or retained.
Personal data will be shared with another system or organisation.
Users need to view, manage, or withdraw previously given consent.
do_not_use_when: Data processing is required by law for a government task or statutory function.
Processing is necessary to deliver a service under authority or contract.
Individuals cannot reasonably be identified from the data.

inputs.label: Consent purpose
inputs.description: The specific reason for requesting consent.

inputs.label: Data description
inputs.description: What data will be accessed or shared.

steps.label: Present consent request
steps.description: Explain what data will be accessed, why, who receives it, and how long it will be used.

steps.label: Record consent decision
steps.description: Record the user's decision with purpose, scope, and timestamp.

steps.label: Handle partial or refused consent
steps.description: Explain what will happen if consent is refused or given partially.

ux_considerations.text: Give users a clear, uncoerced choice to accept or refuse consent.
Get separate consent for each distinct purpose.
Allow users to withdraw consent after it has been given.
Enable save-and-return if users need time to understand what they are agreeing to.

technical_notes.text: Each consent decision must be linked to a specific purpose, associated data, time-bound where applicable, and recorded with date and version.
Systems must prevent processing when consent is refused or withdrawn.
Consent records must support auditing and compliance reviews.
